sections.paragraph:
inf. f. used of a noun, from the verb יָדַע (like דֵּעַ and דֵּעָה)-

(1) knowledge, knowing, sometimes followed by an acc. Jer 22:16, הַדַּעַת אֹתִי knowing me, the knowledge of me; דַּעַת אֱלֹהִים, Hos 4:1 6:6 , and κατʼ ἐξοχὴν, הַדַּעַת Hos 4:6, knowledge (of God). בִּבְלִי דַעַת through ignorance, unawares (opp. to “of set purpose, advisedly”), Deu 4:42 19:4 Jos 20:3. מִבְּלִי דַעַת Isa 5:13, is not “unexpectedly, suddenly” (the interpretation which I defended in my commentary on this passage); but by comparison with Hos 4:6 (where once there is מִבְּלִי הַדַּעַת); “for want of the knowledge of God,” i.e. of religion. Rightly therefore rendered by LXX. διὰ τὸ μὴ εἰδέναι αὐτοὺς τὸν Κύριον. Deu 4:42 19:4 Jos 20:3.

(2) intelligence, understanding, wisdom, i.q. חָכְמָה, תְּבוּנָה Pro 1:4 2:6 24:5 , etc. בִּבְלִי דַעַת foolishly, Job 35:16 36:12 38:2 42:3 . יָדַע דַּעַת to be possessed of wisdom, Pro 17:27.
